title: Reverse an Array
theory: Reversing an array involves swapping elements from both ends moving towards the center.
algorithm:
1. Start
2. Use two pointers: start=0, end=n-1
3. Swap arr[start] and arr[end]
4. Increment start, decrement end
5. Repeat until start >= end

title: Binary Search Implementation
theory: Binary search targets a sorted array and repeatedly divides the search interval in half.
algorithm:
1. Set low=0, high=n-1
2. Find mid = (low+high)/2
3. If target == mid, return
4. If target < mid, high=mid-1
5. Else low=mid+1

title: Sum of Natural Numbers
theory: Calculate the sum of first N natural numbers using a loop.
algorithm:
1. Start
2. Input N
3. Initialize sum = 0
4. For i from 1 to N, sum = sum + i
5. Print sum

title: OSI vs TCP/IP Reference Models
theory: OSI has 7 layers, TCP/IP has 4. Study the encapsulation process and protocol suites.
algorithm:
1. Select Model
2. View Layer Details
3. Analyze Header Encapsulation

title: CSMA/CD Protocol Simulation
theory: Carrier Sense Multiple Access with Collision Detection is a network protocol for carrier transmission.
algorithm:
1. Check if channel is idle
2. If idle, transmit data
3. If collision, wait random backoff
4. Retransmit

title: Introduction to DBMS (DDL/DML)
theory: Learn SQL commands: CREATE, INSERT, SELECT, UPDATE, DELETE.
algorithm:
1. CREATE TABLE students...
2. INSERT INTO students...
3. SELECT * FROM students

title: Relational Algebra Operations
theory: Select, Project, Union, and Join operations in relational databases.

title: Study and Verification of Logic Gates
theory: Verify AND, OR, NOT, NAND, NOR, XOR gates using truth tables.
algorithm:
1. Set Inputs A and B
2. Measure Output Y
3. Compare with Theoretical Truth Table

title: Class and Object Basics
theory: Understand encapsulation and how objects are instantiated from classes in Java.
algorithm:
1. Define Class Person
2. Define attribute 'name'
3. Create Object p = new Person()
4. Set p.name = 'Vaibhav'

title: 8-bit Addition & Subtraction
theory: Write 8085 assembly code to add two 8-bit numbers stored in memory.
algorithm:
1. LXI H, 2000H
2. MOV A, M
3. INX H
4. ADD M
5. STA 2050H
6. HLT

subject: Design and Analysis of Algorithms
title: Insertion Sort Time Complexity Analysis
aim: To analyze time complexity of insertion sort algorithm.
theory: Insertion sort builds sorted array one element at a time by inserting elements into correct position. It works similarly to the way you sort playing cards in your hands. The array is virtually split into a sorted and an unsorted part. Values from the unsorted part are picked and placed at the correct position in the sorted part.
algorithm: Shift larger elements to the right
